request.question: Ex Mach Tina was the 8th episode of the series created by whom?

title: Bob's Burgers
Beneath (The Secret Circle)
Kelly Johnson (engineer)
A Reptile Dysfunction
Ex Mach Tina
The Son Also Rises (Grimm)
Flashes Before Your Eyes
The Van Buren Boys
Bye Bye Boo Boo
Elegy for a Pig
sentences: Bob's Burgers is an American adult animated sitcom created by Loren Bouchard that premiered on Fox on January 9, 2011.
The series centers on the Belchers – parents Bob and Linda, and their children Tina, Gene, and Louise – who run a hamburger restaurant.
The show was conceived by Bouchard after he developed "Home Movies".
It is produced and distributed in association with 20th Century Fox Television.
"Beneath" is the 8th episode of the first season of the CW television series "The Secret Circle", and the series' 8th episode overall.
It was aired on November 3, 2011.
The episode was written by Don Whitehead & Holly Henderson and it was directed by John Fawcett.
Clarence Leonard "Kelly" Johnson (February 27, 1910 – December 21, 1990) was an American aeronautical and systems engineer.
He is recognized for his contributions to a series of important aircraft designs, most notably the Lockheed U-2 and SR-71 Blackbird.

Besides the first production aircraft to exceed Mach 3, he also produced the first fighter capable of Mach 2, the United States' first operational jet fighter, as well as the first U.S. fighter to exceed 400 mph, and many other contributions to a large number of aircraft.
As a member and first team leader of the Lockheed Skunk Works, Johnson worked for more than four decades and is said to have been an "organizing genius".

He played a leading role in the design of over forty aircraft, including several honored with the prestigious Collier Trophy, acquiring a reputation as one of the most talented and prolific aircraft design engineers in the history of aviation.

In 2003, as part of its commemoration of the 100th anniversary of the Wright Brothers' flight, "Aviation Week & Space Technology" ranked Johnson 8th on its list of the top 100 "most important, most interesting, and most influential people" in the first century of aerospace.
Hall Hibbard, Johnson's Lockheed boss, referring to Johnson's Swedish ancestry once remarked to Ben Rich: "That damned Swede can actually see air."
"A Reptile Dysfunction" is the 8th episode of season 5 of the supernatural drama television series "Grimm" and the 96th episode overall, which premiered on February 5, 2016, on the cable network NBC.
The episode was written by Michael Golamco and was directed by David Straiton.

In the episode, Nick meets Meisner and Trubel for information about Hadrian's Wall and the purpose of the organization while also investigating a murder in a lake, which is rumored to be the place for a monster living in the lake.
"Ex Mach Tina" is the 8th episode of the seventh season of the American animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" and the 115th episode overall.
It was written by Greg Thompson and directed by Brian Loschiavo.

Its guest stars are David Herman as Mr. Frond and as Mr. Branca, Robert Smigel as the school's night security guard, Samantha Bee as school nurse Liz, Jenny Slate as Tammy Larsen and Bobby Tisdale as Zeke.
It originally aired in the US on FOX Network at January 8, 2017.
In this episode Tina hurts her ankle and uses a telecommunication robot for school, so she can stay at home.
Jimmy Jr. develops a relationship to the robot version of Tina.
Meanwhile Bob buys a banjo and waits for a good moment to play it.
"The Son Also Rises" is the 8th episode of season 6 of the supernatural drama television series "Grimm" and the 118th episode overall, which premiered on February 24, 2017, on the cable network NBC.
The episode was written by Todd Milliner and Nick Peet and was directed by Peter Werner.
In the episode, Eve is attacked from the other side of the mirror and Nick decides to stay with her.
Meanwhile, Hank and Wu investigate the murders of scientists, discovering that the murders are related to an experiment to resurrect a man's son.
"Flashes Before Your Eyes" is the 8th episode of the third season of the American drama television series "Lost", and the show's 57th episode overall.
The episode was written by the series co-creator, show runner and executive producer Damon Lindelof and supervising producer Drew Goddard, and directed by Jack Bender.
It first aired in the United States on February 14, 2007, on the American Broadcasting Company.
The episode received mostly positive reviews from critics.
Lindelof and Goddard were nominated for the Writers Guild of America (WGA) Award for Best Episodic Drama at the February 2008 ceremony for writing the episode.
"The Van Buren Boys" is the 148th episode of the sitcom "Seinfeld", and name of a fictional New York street gang.
Their sign is one hand with all the fingers and thumb 'up' and spread out.
The other hand has all but the thumb and pointer finger up, for a total of eight (8).
The gang is named for the 8th President of the United States, Martin Van Buren (who was the first president from New York).
This was the 14th episode for the 8th season.
It aired on February 6, 1997.
"Bye Bye Boo Boo" is the 16th episode of the sixth season of the animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" and the overall 104th episode, and is written by Scott Jacobson and directed by Tyree Dillihay.
It aired on Fox in the United States on May 8, 2016.
In the episode, after hearing that Boo Boo is leaving Boyz 4 Now, Louise enters a contest that could finally give Tina a chance to meet him.
Meanwhile, Bob and Linda learn about a mobster who got shot in the restaurant in 1931, which causes tension between Bob and Jimmy Pesto.
"Log 105: Elegy for a Pig" (a.k.a. simply "Elegy for a Pig") is the 8th episode of the 3rd season of the American television series "Adam-12", the 60th episode of the series overall.
Originally aired on November 21, 1970, the episode focuses on the life of a recently killed police officer.

The episode's format is a major departure from the series' usual format; instead of presenting a day in the life of two officers, it presents a first-person narration from the point of view of one of the series' main protagonists.
It is presented as a tribute to police officers who have been killed in the line of duty.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Ex Mach Tina was the 8th episode of the series created by whom?
hop1_docs: Ex Mach Tina | "Ex Mach Tina" is the 8th episode of the seventh season of the American animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" and the 115th episode overall.  It was written by Greg Thompson and directed by Brian Loschiavo.  Its guest stars are David Herman as Mr. Frond and as Mr. Branca, Robert Smigel as the school's night security guard, Samantha Bee as school nurse Liz, Jenny Slate as Tammy Larsen and Bobby Tisdale as Zeke.  It originally aired in the US on FOX Network at January 8, 2017.  In this episode Tina hurts her ankle and uses a telecommunication robot for school, so she can stay at home.  Jimmy Jr. develops a relationship to the robot version of Tina.  Meanwhile Bob buys a banjo and waits for a good moment to play it.

Mach a Šebestová | Mach a Šebestová (in English: Mach and Šebestová or Max and Sally) is a Czech cartoon series.  It was created by writer Miloš Macourek, director Jaroslav Doubrava, and animator Adolf Born.  The animated characters were voiced by Petr Nárožný.  The story follows the adventures of two third-year pupils and a torn off telephone receiver.  The first series of 13 episodes was broadcast on Czechoslovak Television in 1982.  Mach a Šebestová is one of the most popular serials made for children's television programme Večerníček.  The cartoon was adapted for the film "Mach, Šebestová a kouzelné sluchátko" (Max, Sally and the Magic Phone, 2001) by director Václav Vorlíček.  In 2010, "Theatre Lampion" presented a stage adaptation of the series in the Czech National theatre.

List of Trailer Park Boys episodes | "Trailer Park Boys" is a Canadian mockumentary television series created and directed by Mike Clattenburg and a continuation of Clattenburg's 1999 film of the same name.  The series focuses on the misadventures of a group of trailer park residents, some of whom are ex-convicts, living in the fictional Sunnyvale Trailer Park in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia.

The Equestranauts | "The Equestranauts" is the 17th episode of the fourth season of the American animated television series "Bob's Burgers", and the 62nd episode overall.  It was written by Dan Mintz and directed by Tyree Dillihay, and was released on April 13, 2014.  Parodying the "" fanbase of the animated children's cartoon "", the episode's plot centers on Tina discovering her favorite animated pony cartoon has a fanbase of adult men, one of whom tricks Tina out of her rare toy pony, forcing Bob to go undercover and try to get it back.

List of Crazy Ex-Girlfriend episodes | "Crazy Ex-Girlfriend" is an American musical comedy-drama television series created by Rachel Bloom and Aline Brosh McKenna, that premiered on The CW on October 12, 2015.  The series stars Bloom as Rebecca Bunch, a depressed young woman who decides to follow her ex-boyfriend from New York City to West Covina, California in the hopes of finding real happiness.  All episode titles follow the pattern of referring to Rebecca's ex-boyfriend, Josh.  On March 11, 2016, The CW renewed the series for a second season which premiered on October 21, 2016.

Sea Me Now | "Sea Me Now" is the second episode of the seventh season of the animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" and the overall 109th episode, and is written by Dan Fybel and directed by Chris Song.  It aired on Fox in the United States on October 9, 2016.  In the episode, Teddy tries to impress his ex-wife, Denise, when the Belchers accompany him for a ride on his newly restored boat.  Meanwhile, Tina takes care of Bob's restaurant eraser, in an attempt to get her own cell phone.

List of 30 Rock episodes | "30 Rock" is an American satirical television sitcom that ran on NBC from October 11, 2006, to January 31, 2013.  Created by Tina Fey, the series follows the lives of the head writer of "The Girlie Show with Tracy Jordan" (TGS), Liz Lemon (Tina Fey), the other staff members of "TGS", and their network executive, Jack Donaghy (Alec Baldwin).  A total of 138 episodes of "30 Rock" were produced and aired over seven seasons.
hop1_titles: List of Trailer Park Boys episodes
response.summary_1: Passage [1] states: "'Ex Mach Tina' is the 8th episode of the seventh season of the American animated comedy series 'Bob's Burgers'" (originally aired January 8, 2017). Missing fact: the creator(s) of the series "Bob's Burgers." Next retrieval direction: look up the "Bob's Burgers" series page or sources for "Bob's Burgers" creator (use anchors "Bob's Burgers" and the episode title "Ex Mach Tina").
hop2_docs: Loren Bouchard | Loren Hal Bouchard (born October 10, 1969) is an American voice actor, animator, writer, producer, television director and composer.  He is best known for several animated TV shows and as a co-creator of "Home Movies" with Brendon Small.  He is also the creator of "Bob's Burgers" and "Lucy, the Daughter of the Devil".

Burger Wars (Bob's Burgers) | "Burger Wars" is the tenth episode of the first season of the animated television series "Bob's Burgers".  The episode originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on April 10, 2011.
List of Bob's Burgers characters | The following is a list of characters from American animated television series "Bob's Burgers".
Bob's Burgers (season 8) | The eighth season of the animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" began airing on Fox in the United States on October 1, 2017.

Bob's Burgers (season 4) | The fourth season of the animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" began airing on Fox in the United States on September 29, 2013, and concluded on May 18, 2014.  The show was once again part of Fox's Sunday night lineup, initially airing in its usual timeslot of 8:30 PM Eastern.  Beginning on March 9 and continuing for the rest of the season, "Bob's Burgers" moved to 7:00 PM Eastern; the move came as a result of the addition of "" to the network's lineup.
Bob's Burgers (season 5) | The fifth season of the animated comedy series "Bob's Burgers" began airing on Fox in the United States on October 5, 2014, and concluded on May 17, 2015.
Bob's Burgers (season 3) | The third season of the animated sitcom "Bob's Burgers" began airing on Fox in the United States on September 30, 2012, and concluded on May 12, 2013.
response.summary_2: "Ex Mach Tina" is the eighth episode of the seventh season of the American animated comedy series Bob's Burgers. The series was created by Loren Bouchard (passage [1]).
